standouts: The quoted prior paragraph uses the metaphor: atoms of the graph exist and run; the molecule (wiring them into a walkable, scoped, observation-bearing web) is the declared-but-unbuilt next step; the dragnet is the natural first thing to pour into it
Tim's counter-reminder: 'you need to assume that it is not right. It is not finished. It's not done'

intention_signals: To push back against premature acceptance of a design as complete or correct, and to reinforce a rule that current work should always be assumed unfinished and potentially wrong.
